hook:
The $0 to $1k/mo app playbook nobody talks about:

No coding bootcamp.
No $50k in VC funding.
No team.

Just one person with Claude and 14 days.
tweets:
Step 1: Find a proven app.

Go to AppFigures or Sensor Tower.

Filter: $50k-$200k/month revenue, <1000 reviews.

These apps print money but have weak marketing. They're clonable.

Step 2: Pick a niche angle.

Don't clone exactly. Find an underserved audience.

Example: Habit tracker app doing $100k/mo.

Your version: Habit tracker for Muslim prayer times.
Or: Habit tracker for gym bros.
Or: Habit tracker for new moms.

Step 3: Find MIT-licensed repos.

GitHub search: "habit tracker" license:mit stars:>100

You'll find 50+ open source versions.

Fork one. Customize the UI. Add your niche features.

Legal. Free. Fast.

Step 4: Build in 14 days max.

Week 1: Core functionality working
Week 2: Polish UI, add paywall

Use Expo + React Native.
RevenueCat for subscriptions.
Ship to TestFlight by day 10.

Step 5: The $4.99/week paywall trick.

Price at $4.99/week with 3-day trial.

That's $20/month effective price.

50 subscribers = $1,000/month.

Most apps price too low. Don't.

Step 6: Screenshots matter more than features.

Spend 2 hours on App Store screenshots.

Show the app solving a problem. Text overlay with the benefit.

First 2 screenshots = 80% of conversion.

Step 7: Launch and iterate.

Week 3: Submit to App Store
Week 4: Post on Reddit (niche subreddit), ProductHunt, X

First 100 users = feedback goldmine.

Fix what they complain about. Double down on what they love.

Real numbers from my last app:

Day 1-7: 23 downloads
Day 8-14: 89 downloads (Reddit post hit)
Day 15-30: 340 downloads

Month 1 revenue: $847
Month 2 revenue: $1,240
Month 3 revenue: $1,890

The unsexy truth: most apps fail.

But apps fail because people spend 6 months building and 0 days marketing.

Flip it. 14 days building. Then 14 days marketing.

Ship fast. Learn what works. Iterate.
cta:
I'm building 3 apps this quarter using this exact playbook.

Following along? Drop a comment.

DM "APP" for my App Store screenshot templates.
